programme.title: Christian McBride
programme.desc: On July 20, 2018, double bassist Christian McBride presented his band ‘New Jawn' at the Malta Jazz Festival. A five-time Grammy winner, McBride is one of the most requested, most recorded, and most respected figures in the music world today. Hailing from Philadelphia, this music luminary combines jazz, R&B, pop/rock, hip hop/neo-soul, and classical. Gracing the Malta Jazz stage with him are Nasheet Waits (drums), Marcus Strickland (tenor sax), and Josh Evans (trumpet).

programme.title: Dhafer Youssef
programme.desc: Jazz à Vienne is one of the world's most prestigious jazz festivals. Ever since 1981, it has attracted a mix of jazz legends and exciting newcomers, with many of them playing return engagements. Each year welcomes outstanding international stars to the historical Le Théâtre Antique to impress and inspire their audiences. In 2018, Tunisian composer Dhafer Youssef alternated between his hypnotic voice and masterful oud playing at Jazz à Vienne. His stunning abilities have to be heard to be believed. He is joined by bassist Matt Brewer and drummer Aaron Parks.

programme.title: Chucho Valdis
programme.desc: Legendary Cuban pianist Chucho Valdés brought his band ‘Jazz Batá 2' to the Malta Jazz Festival on July 19, 2019. The air was thick with anticipation as Valdés took to the stage, revisiting the small-group concept of his mythical 1972 Cuban album ‘Jazz Batá'. The batá repertoire – the deep classical music of West Africa – permeates Valdés' piano solos throughout the concert. Valdés's band consists of Dreiser Durruthy (Batas and vocals), Yaroldy Abreu (percussions and vocals), Abraham Mansfarroll (percussion), Ramón Vazquez Martirena-Bajo (bass guitar). They delivered a performance that reverberated through the historic island.

programme.title: Al Jarreau, Tom Canning, Peter Robinson & Ralph Humphrey
programme.sub-title: Al Jarreau - The North Sea Jazz
programme.desc: The North Sea Jazz Festival is the largest indoor music festival in the world, known globally as the event where the past, present and future of jazz are featured within three days. Next to a firm base of jazz as the festival's staple music genre, many others, such as blues, soul, funk, or hip hop, pass by. In 1981, American singer and Grammy Award winner Al Jarreau gave an unforgettable performance for the audience of the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague, the Netherlands.

programme.title: Jimmy Smith
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: Chet Baker
programme.desc: This selection of unforgettable performances by some of the most iconic and influential male jazz and pop vocalists in history showcases these legends' unique essence. Delight in the expressive beauty and emotional depth of Chet Baker's rendition of “Time After Time” and “You Don't Know What Love Is", recorded live in Brussels, Belgium in 1964 and in Rome, Italy in 1956 respectively. Revel in Frank Sinatra's iconic version of “I've Got You Under My Skin". Immerse yourself in Dizzy Gillespie's dynamic performance of “Let Me Outta Here”, recorded live in Denmark in 1970. Enjoy Tony Bennett's unforgettable performance of “It Don't Mean A Thing (If It Ain't Got That Swing)” at London's majestic Royal Albert Hall in 1974. Let the haunting beauty of Nat King Cole's 1957 performance of “Autumn Leaves” and Louis Armstrong's performance of “Basin Street Blues” from 1959 delight you. Dean Martin's smooth vocal stylings will enthrall you as he performs the jazz standard “Wrap Your Troubles In Dreams". Witness the electrifying energy of Sammy Davis Jr.'s performance of “Too Close For Comfort”. Join us for this unforgettable musical experience that you'll surely cherish!

programme.title: Count Basie
programme.desc: The North Sea Jazz Festival is the largest indoor music festival in the world, known globally as the event where the past, present and future of jazz are featured within three days. Next to a firm base of jazz as the festival's staple music genre, many others, such as blues, soul, funk, or hip hop, pass by. In 1979, legendary big band leader and pianist Count Basie brought his famous big band to the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague to perform two sets. Basie, whose minimalist pianism and blues-oriented swing style had cemented his popularity as early as the 1930s, was in the midst of a European tour and had recorded his live album On the Road the previous night in Montreux, Switzerland. Here is the first of two sets recorded at the festival in 1979.

programme.title: Miles Davis
programme.desc: American trumpeter Miles Davis (1926-1991) was a pioneering jazz musician from the late 1940s to the late 1980s. His influence on 20th century music is hard to overstate, as he trailblazed bebop, cool jazz, hardbop, modal jazz, and electronic music. On October 11, 1964, Miles Davis performed at Teatro Dell'Arte in Milan. With tenor saxophonist Wayne Shorter, pianist Herbie Hancock, bassist Ron Carter and drummer Tony Williams, Davis performed Autumn Leaves, My Funny Valentine, All Blues, All of You, and Joshua.

programme.title: Alex Dee
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: Count Basie
programme.desc: The North Sea Jazz Festival is the largest indoor music festival in the world, known globally as the event where the past, present and future of jazz are featured within three days. Next to a firm base of jazz as the festival's staple music genre, many others, such as blues, soul, funk, or hip hop, pass by. In 1979, legendary big band leader and pianist Count Basie brought his famous big band to the North Sea Jazz Festival in The Hague to perform two sets. Basie, whose minimalist pianism and blues-oriented swing style had cemented his popularity as early as the 1930s, was in the midst of a European tour and had recorded his live album 'On the Road' the previous night in Montreux, Switzerland. Here is the second of two sets recorded at the festival in 1979.

programme.title: Kirill Karabits, Russian National Orchestra & Mikhail Pletnev
programme.desc: Pianist Mikhail Pletnev is accompanied by the Russian National Orchestra led by Kirill Karabits in a performance of Camille Saint-Saëns's Piano Concerto No. 2, Op. 22. At its premiere in 1868, the orchestra was led by Anton Rubinstein, with the composer himself at the piano. The current concert was recorded at the Moscow Philharmonic Society and opens with a performance of Sergei Prokofiev's symphonic picture 'Dreams'. The concert was part of the Ninth Russian National Orchestra Grand Festival from 2017.

programme.title: Seong-Jin Cho
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Gewandhausorchester, Andris Nelsons & Baiba Skride
programme.desc: Andris Nelsons's starts off his official opening concert as principal conductor of the Gewandhausorchester with the world premiere of Steffen Schleiermacher's 'Relief for Orchestra'. Baiba Skride features as soloist in Alban Berg's Violin Concerto. In the slow finale the solo violin's line is gradually joined by the rest of the first violin, providing a lovely moment between soloist and orchestra. Skride's encore is Imitatione delle Campane by von Westhoff, a Bach contemporary. The concert closes with a rendition of Mendelssohn Symphony No. 3, Op. 56, known as the 'Scottish' which actually premiered in the Leipzig Gewandhaus in 1842. Recorded on February 22/23, 2018 in the Gewandhaus, Leipzig.

programme.title: Mahler Chamber Orchestra & Barbara Hannigan
programme.sub-title: Barbara Hannigan: A Late Night Concert
programme.desc: Barbara Hannigan conducts the Mahler Chamber Orchestra in a concert recorded at the Lucerne Festival of 2014. There are pianists who also conduct, and concert masters who lead their orchestra from the violin desk, but a star soprano who coordinates an ensemble while at the same time negotiating the trickiest coloratura singing is something entirely new! This concert opens with the Overture from Rossini's La scala di seta, followed by three concert arias by Mozart: Vado, ma dove? O Dei!, K. 583, Un moto di gioia, K. 579 and Misera, dove son? K. 369. After renditions of György Ligeti's Concert Românesc and Mysteries of the Macabre, the concert closes with Fauré's Pelléas et Mélisande, Op. 80, a suite derived from incidental music.

programme.title: Julie Berthollet, Camille Berthollet, Guillaume Vincent & Ensemble Appassionato
programme.sub-title: Berthollet Sisters at Théâtre des Champs-Élysées
programme.desc: For this exceptional recital, recorded in 2019 at the Théâtre des Champs-Élysées, Camille (cello) and Julie Berthollet (violin) are accompanied by pianist Guillaume Vincent and the Ensemble Appassionato under the direction of Mathieu Herzog. The two musical sisters have chosen a repertoire which crosses genres and eras to explore the expressive palette of the violin and cello, from the baroque of Vivaldi to the jazzy notes of Django Reinhardt and Stéphane Grapelli. Among the works on the program are Brahms Hungarian Dances No. 5 and No. 6, Karl Jenkins' Palladio, 'Theme from Forrest Gump' by Alan Silvestri, Schumann's Piano Quintet, Op. 44, The Red Violin by Corigliano, Hungarian Rhapsody, op. 68 by Popper, Minor Swing by Reinhardt/Grappeli and Csárdás by Monti.

programme.title: Sir Simon Rattle & London Symphony Orchestra
programme.sub-title: The LSO performs Dvořák and Tchaikovsky
programme.desc: Sir Simon Rattle conducts the London Symphony Orchestra (LSO) in a concert program consisting of Antonín Dvořák's Scherzo Capriccioso in D-flat major, Op. 66, and selections from Act II of Pyotr Ilyich Tchaikovsky's famous ballet The Nutcracker (1892). Dvořák's Scherzo Capriccioso (1883) reflects a period of personal crisis for the composer. The work betrays ever-changing moods and a constant sense of inner restlessness. In the music of The Nutcracker, Tchaikovsky combined memorable melodies with colorful orchestration, which has enchanted listeners for decades. This performance was recorded at LSO St Luke's in London, UK, on March 18, 2021.

programme.title: Snow Patrol
programme.desc: With their third album, 'Final Straw', selling a cool 1.5 million in the UK in its year of release, Snow Patrol's reputation as a remarkable band remains intact. 'Live At Somerset House' reveals why the band is praised for delivering fantastic live performances. It also places them at one of London's most gorgeous settings which complements the band's distinctive and provocative sound perfectly. Performing live for the first time with a string section, this amazing Snow Patrol concert, features all the key hits including 'Run', 'Spitting Games', 'Chocolate' and a blistering version of 'Tiny Little Fractures'.

programme.title: Paul Simon
programme.desc: During the first leg of Paul Simon's triumphant, 2011 U.S. tour, he thrilled hometown fans with a special club performance at New York City's historic Webster Hall. The show presents this iconic artist working at the peak of his artistic and creative powers in an unusually intimate setting. The 20-song, 90-minute set list was drawn from his legendary career and includes several songs that haven't been performed live in many years. "Kodachrome," "Mother and Child Reunion," "Gone At Last" and "The Obvious Child" are just some of the highlights as well as songs from Simon's most recent album, 2011's So Beautiful or So What (Hear Music/Concord Music Group) including "Dazzling Blue," "Rewrite," "The Afterlife" and the album's propulsive title track.

programme.title: James Morrison
programme.desc: T in the Park festival is a major Scottish music festival that has been held almost annually since 1994 (the event did not take place in 2017). It is named after its main sponsor, the brewing company Tennents.

programme.title: The Lumineers
programme.desc: The Lumineers bring energetic American folk-rock to the picturesque Musilac Festival. You won't be able to resist clapping along and stomping your feet to the band's raw sound and enchanting music.

programme.title: David Gilmour
programme.desc: David Gilmour - Remember That Night: Live at the Royal Albert Hall is recorded by guitarist David Gilmour where he presents live tracks from the album On an Island and some hits from his former band Pink Floyd.

programme.title: Flying Over Spanish Islands
programme.desc: In their first collaboration, cinematographer Stefan Zimmerman and Nature Relaxation™ producer David Huting present "Above the Spanish Islands." This 50-minute 4K aerial film captures Mallorca and the Canary Islands, set to ambient music by Diamans, Electus, and Michael St. Laurent.

programme.title: Flying Over Iceland
programme.desc: "Flying Over Iceland: Part I" provides a soothing aerial journey through eastern Iceland's fjords and valleys, starting at sunrise in Brei_dalsvik. Enjoy views of mists, a waterfall, and the Brei_dals' river, set to nature sounds and music by The Cynic Project, showcasing East Iceland's beauty.

programme.title: Flying Over Iceland II
programme.desc: Summertime in Iceland from an elevated perspective with "Flying Over Iceland II: Summer in the Golden Circle."

programme.title: Flying Over Kauai
programme.desc: "Flying Over Kauai" provides a soothing aerial journey over Hawaii's Garden Island, featuring the lush NaPali Coast, waterfalls, canyons, and beaches. Paired with new music by The Cynic Project and nature sounds, this 4K film offers a visually and sonically immersive experience.

programme.title: Flying Over Oahu
programme.desc: "Flying Over Oahu" offers a 97-minute aerial journey over Oahu's iconic coastlines with nature sounds and music by Alex Smith (The Cynic Project). From Waikiki Beach to Turtle Bay, enjoy breathtaking 4K landscapes, ending with a stunning Waikiki sunset.

programme.title: A Day in Santorini
programme.desc: Turn any screen into a window the the Greek Islands with this immensely relaxing HD visual journey through the unique and beautiful coastal village of Oia, found on the far tip of Santorini Island. Slowly awaken your senses as the morning sun rises over the sea, and watch as day slowly passes into dusk. Distant waves break and birds chirp adding an a nice ambient music that combines to create a video that's so relaxing it's almost trance-inducing.

programme.title: A Day in the Caribbean 3
programme.desc: A Day in the Caribbean transports viewers miles away from it all, to the gorgeous tropical island of Antigua. Arriving just at dawn, you'll enjoy a stunning golden sunrise and rainbow, followed by a couple hours of beautiful beach scenes as you explore the beautiful resort surroundings. Abundant, vibrant vistas will instantly transform your screen into a window to the glorious Caribbean - with nothing but the pure sounds of nature to accompany it. (Music version coming soon though)

programme.title: Patagonia Mountain Stream
programme.desc: Transform any TV or digital screen into a rejuvenating, refreshing window to the glacial meltwater springs of Chile's famous Torres Del Paine National Park in Patagonia with this 1-hour long fixed-angle nature relaxation™ video complete with stereo nature sounds of the creek and birds chirping.

programme.title: Lush Vineyards: Portugal
programme.desc: Escape to the jaw-droppingly beautiful Douro Valley, Portugal's most scenic wine regions.

programme.title: Shark Paradise
programme.desc: Leave your worries from the world above behind and dive into a vibrant undersea world inhabited by the iconic sharks, for 80 minutes of Nature Relaxation. Captured by filmmaker collaborator Peter Schneider, Shark Paradise features hundreds of unique real-time scenes captured from around the world, paired with gentle underwater sounds & calming music for deep relaxation by Relax Moods.

programme.title: Islands of Paradise w Music
programme.desc: Transform any TV or digital screen into a window to the beautiful Yasawa islands of Fiji, with this one hour-long, soothing visual journey. Palm trees, tropical lagoons, birds chirping, waves lapping, golden sunsets and fiery sunrises are just a few of the scenes in store for the viewer. Enjoy nearly all the benefits of a relaxing tropical vacation from the comfort of your home!

programme.title: A Day in Big Sur 1
programme.desc: Transport your senses to the rugged yet exquisitely beautiful Big Sur coast of California to relax & absorb the healing sights and sounds of McWay Falls from a unique and rare perspective. Filmed more than a decade ago and remastered for maximum color and quality, "A Day in Big Sur" showcases the timeless beauty of this magical destination and attempts to truly capture the spirit of this natural paradise through the use of 50+ artistically framed video perspectives - all featuring real stereo nature sounds for an immersive relaxation experience.

programme.title: ART Escape
programme.desc: Discover the artistic pulse that beats within the soul of the city! Captivating cityscapes and skylines, alive with movement and bold architectural beauty, showcase the essence of modern life and exude the electric energy through the vibrant hues.

programme.title: ART Escape
programme.desc: From mountain peaks to coastal shores, explore Canada's vast beauty. This collection of stunning cityscapes and wild landscapes takes you on a visual journey through the Great White North, satisfying your wanderlust from the comfort of home.

programme.title: ART Escape
programme.desc: From London's iconic landmarks to the rolling hills of the Cotswolds and the ancient stones of Stonehenge, explore England's rich tapestry. This visual journey captures the timeless charm of historic cities and the serene beauty of the countryside.

programme.title: ARTspiration
programme.desc: Celebrate the beauty of spring through the luminous brushstrokes of Impressionism. Featuring works by contemporary artists & old masters, the art is accompanied by contemporary classical music, immersing viewers in the colors & rhythms of the season.

programme.title: ARTspiration
programme.desc: Self-taught artist MJ On The Wall blends geometry, afro-futurism, and color symbolism to explore the African diaspora's past, present, and future. Discover how the engineer-turned-artist tells long-form stories through his intricate narratives.

programme.title: ARTspiration
programme.desc: A fusion of the diverse artistic styles & visions of our featured artists. Erni Vales unleashes imagination with surreal creations that transport you to another world while Sam Heydt challenges perspectives with thought-provoking conceptual art.

programme.title: ARTspiration
programme.desc: Explore the floral motion art of Jennifer Panepinto (QuantumSpirit). Her work blends AI and digital editing to explore memory and consciousness through bold color, intricate texture, and symbolic motifs. A visually textured, emotionally resonant collection.

programme.title: ARTspiration
programme.desc: Experience the abstract motion art of Dennis Miller, a visual artist with a composer's background. He translates musical principles like rhythm and structure into imagery, transforming photographs into emotionally deep, non-representational works of art.

programme.title: ART Buzz
programme.desc: Step into a realm of electrifying fashion and avant-garde creativity with this high-energy collection. Unleashing the boundless imagination with cutting-edge art that redefines fashion's limits and leaves an indelible mark on the world of style.

programme.title: ART Buzz
programme.desc: A vibrant collection celebrating the creativity of Asian Pacific Islander Desi American artists. Blending ancestral influences with modern expression, each piece captures the dynamic intersection of tradition, identity, and the evolving artistic landscape.
